ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no lamb-specific dumpling entry exists. Mapped to the closest broader class FOODON_03315671 (dumpling food product).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EKP covers meat-filled dumplings broadly; no lamb-specific sub-code exists. Used as closest defensible analogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for lamb dumplings as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 dumplings, approximately 90g total, as described on the menu (3 pcs).

nutrient_source.title: House Special Lamb Dumplings — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for House Special Lamb Dumplings (cr_id G00010688). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB record; saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lamb mince (cooked) and wheat dumpling wrapper composite; dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central for comparable meat-filled dumplings.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for meat-filled dumplings / wheat-based dim sum
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lamb dumplings. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for steamed meat-filled dumplings and wheat-based dim sum items (GI range 40–50). The wheat dumpling wrapper is the primary glycaemic driver; the high protein and fat content from lamb mince further attenuates the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (7.2g from 90g portion × 8.03g/100g) / 100 = 3.2.

hypertension.guidance: At 46mg sodium per 100g, these dumplings are well below the 200mg/100g threshold for hypertension concern; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.0g/100g), high protein (8.7g/100g), very low sugar (0.2g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The favourable protein-to-carbohydrate ratio and very low sugar content support glycaemic stability; patients should limit portion size to 3 pieces per serving, avoid sweet dipping sauces, and prefer steamed over pan-fried preparation to minimise fat-driven insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from lamb mince and cholesterol 28mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lamb mince contributes saturated fat (estimated 1.5g/100g) and cholesterol (28mg/100g); patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 3 pieces and prefer steamed preparation, while avoiding butter- or oil-enriched dipping sauces that add further saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), a pro-inflammatory precursor
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lamb is a primary dietary concern for rheumatoid arthritis due to its arachidonic acid content, which amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; patients with RA should consider substituting with chicken or prawn dumplings and avoid soy-sauce-heavy dipping sauces that add sodium and worsen tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: House Special Lamb Dumplings are handcrafted Chinese-style dumplings filled with seasoned lamb mince, typically flavoured with ginger, spring onion, and sesame oil, enclosed in a thin wheat-flour wrapper. Served as a portion of 3 pieces (approximately 90g), they are most commonly steamed, though pan-frying is an optional preparation. The texture is tender and juicy with a delicate savoury flavour profile characteristic of Northern Chinese lamb dumpling traditions. Nutritionally, per 100g they provide 101 kcal, 8.7g protein, 3.8g fat, and 8.0g carbohydrate with very low sugar (0.2g) and exceptionally low sodium (46mg). The high protein-to-carbohydrate ratio makes them suitable for glycaemic management in Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without sweet dipping sauces. The low sodium content makes them suitable for hypertension, though dipping sauces remain the primary sodium risk. The saturated fat and cholesterol from lamb mince warrant moderate caution for cardiovascular disease patients. For rheumatoid arthritis, lamb's arachidonic acid content is a pro-inflammatory concern. These dumplings are a popular dim sum and meal item in Singapore's Chinese food culture, suitable for lunch and dinner occasions. They are halal-friendly and dairy-free but contain gluten and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010688) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for comparable meat-filled dumplings; these are clearly documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed meat-filled wheat dumplings.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, most vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific lamb dumpling preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. Saturated fat (1.5g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked ground lamb and wheat dumpling wrapper composite (USDA FDC ID 174351 and 169715). Dietary fibre (0.8g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central for comparable steamed meat-filled dumplings. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed meat-filled wheat dumplings; no direct GI measurement for lamb dumplings exists in the approved GI database. GL per serving = 45 × (90g × 8.03/100) / 100 = 3.2. All core macros (protein_g 8.71, fat_g 3.78, carbohydrate_g 8.03) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
